responseMessageContents.text:
The local changes are complete and tests pass, but the GitHub push is blocked because the device authorization codes expired without being confirmed. I notice there's a GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable available that I could use as an alternative authentication method instead of waiting for device code confirmation.

Let me verify the current sandbox state to confirm everything's still intact, check if the device code was approved in the meantime, and then try using the requestEnvs tool to ask for a fresh GitHub Personal Access Token as a cleaner alternative to the device flow.

input.command: ls /tmp/gh_token 2>/dev/null && echo "TOKEN PRESENT" || echo "no token"; ls /tmp/gh_device.json 2>/dev/null || echo "device json gone"; cd /home/user/rork-app && git status --porcelain | rg "backend/(services|api)"; bun test backend/services/ivx-deployment-claim-gate.test.ts 2>&1 | tail -3

output.stdout:
no token
/tmp/gh_device.json
 0 fail
 43 expect() calls
Ran 23 tests across 1 file. [71.00ms]

responseMessageContents.inputArgsStr: {"command": "ls /tmp/gh_token 2>/dev/null && echo \"TOKEN PRESENT\" || echo \"no token\"; ls /tmp/gh_device.json 2>/dev/null || echo \"device json gone\"; cd /home/user/rork-app && git status --porcelain | rg \"backend/(services|api)\"; bun test backend/services/ivx-deployment-claim-gate.test.ts 2>&1 | tail -3"}

input.command:
python3 -c "
import json, urllib.request, urllib.parse
try:
    d = json.load(open('/tmp/gh_device.json'))
    data = urllib.parse.urlencode({'client_id': '178c6fc778ccc68e1d6a', 'device_code': d['device_code'], 'grant_type': 'urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:device_code'}).encode()
    req = urllib.request.Request('https://github.com/login/oauth/access_token', data=data, headers={'Accept': 'application/json'})
    r = json.load(urllib.request.urlopen(req))
    if 'access_token' in r:
        open('/tmp/gh_token','w').write(r['access_token']); print('TOKEN OBTAINED')
    else:
        print('status:', r.get('error'))
except FileNotFoundError:
    print('device json wiped')
"

responseMessageContents.text: The device auth flow is stuck waiting for user input that never comes, so the better approach is to request a fresh GitHub Personal Access Token through the environment setup instead of relying on the revoked stored token. I'm noticing the sandbox shows the GITHUB_TOKEN is empty anyway, which confirms we need to get a new one from the user.The gate fix is fully verified locally but can't push to GitHub due to expired device codes and a revoked token. I need you to create a fresh GitHub token so I can run the full deployment chain in one go.

envs.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
envs.description: GitHub Personal Access Token with 'repo' scope for ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest — used to push the deployment-claim-gate fix and trigger the Render deploy. Create at github.com/settings/tokens/new

input.reason: The sandbox's stored GitHub token was revoked and three device-flow authorization codes expired unanswered. A fresh token with repo scope is required to push the verified fix and complete the deployment chain.

responseMessageContents.title: Fix the chat system and update the mobile app settings.
responseMessageContents.description: Corrected the app type to ensure it works properly on mobile devices. Fixed the chat feature so it can talk to the AI again without errors. Added a more reliable way to confirm that updates are actually finished and working correctly.
